# Literature Review

## Current Approaches to Cognitive Decline Prediction

<!--
Review existing studies that use NACC or similar datasets.
Focus on:
- Which features/biomarkers have been shown to predict MCI/AD conversion
- Common ML methods used in the field (logistic regression, random forest, XGBoost)
- How other studies define their groups (NL, MCI, AD transitions)
-->

## The Role of Neuropsychological Testing

<!--
- Which cognitive tests are most sensitive to early decline (e.g., LOGIMEM, Trail Making)
- How composite scores vs. individual tests compare
-->

## Biomarkers in Early Detection

<!--
- APOE ε4 as a genetic risk factor
- CSF biomarkers (amyloid, tau) and their predictive value
- Challenges: small sample sizes, missing data, cost
-->

## Gaps in Existing Research

<!--
Identify what your project contributes or explores differently:
- Combining cognitive, demographic, NPI, and genetic features
- Investigating disease regression (MCI → NL)
- Comparing group-specific feature importance
-->
